# Vectros API — Integration Context

Vectros is the secure data, document, search, and AI back-end that developers
build apps on — a typed data store, a document pipeline, hybrid search,
citation-grounded retrieval, multi-tenant isolation, least-privilege access
control, and an audited compliance posture, behind one coherent API plus a Node
SDK, a CLI, and an MCP server.

The current surface spans: Documents and structured Records (with schema-defined
indexing, lookup/range queries, and folders), hybrid Search (BM25 + vector, RRF
fusion), grounded inference (chat, RAG, document-ask, model catalog), the
Identity plane (users, orgs, clients), per-customer isolation with scoped keys
and app-contexts, read-access logging, GDPR erasure and export, and billing/usage
reporting.

## Recommended integration paths

Most integrations should NOT hand-roll HTTP. Reach for a client:

- **Node SDK (`@vectros-ai/sdk`)** — the typed, recommended path for application
  code. `npm install @vectros-ai/sdk`, construct one client with a token and
  environment, then call sub-clients grouped by area (`records`, `schemas`,
  `documents`, `folders`, `search`, `inference`, `identity`, `auth`). Generated
  from the same spec that drives the API, so method names and types match the API
  exactly. Java and Python are also generated; Node is the verified reference.
- **CLI (`@vectros-ai/cli`, `vectros`)** — provision from a terminal or CI:
  blueprints, app-contexts, identities, scoped keys, and a one-command MCP
  credential bootstrap (`npx -y @vectros-ai/cli bootstrap`). No code, no handling
  your root key directly.
- **MCP server (`@vectros-ai/mcp-server`)** — see below.

## MCP server — call Vectros as native agent tools

Vectros ships an official **Model Context Protocol** server
(`@vectros-ai/mcp-server`) that exposes the data plane to MCP-aware agents
(Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Continue, VS Code, hosted platforms) as callable
tools — search, records, documents, folders, and grounded inference — with no
custom integration code. Drop one entry into the agent's MCP config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "vectros": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@vectros-ai/mcp-server"],
      "env": { "VECTROS_API_KEY": "ssk_live_..." }
    }
  }
}
```

It is a thin shim over the SDK: tenant isolation and scope enforcement hold
exactly as on the API — running the server does not expand a credential's reach.
The tools are data-plane only (no web or third-party tools). Use a scoped
permanent key (`ssk_*`) for desktop installs; the CLI `bootstrap` mints one and
merges the config for you. Full details: https://docs.vectros.ai/guides/clients/mcp/

## Base URL & Auth

- **URL:** `https://api.vectros.ai/v1`
- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- **Root keys (`sk_live_*` / `sk_test_*`):** Long-lived, server-side only. Never
  expose in client-side code or agent prompts.
- **Scoped permanent keys (`ssk_*`):** Least-privilege keys carrying exactly the
  permissions you grant. The right shape for agents and MCP installs.
- **Scoped tokens (`st_*`):** Short-lived JWTs minted via `POST /v1/auth/token`.
  Use for client-side or per-session access. Scoped to specific resources and
  operations.

## Core Architecture & Execution Rules

1. **The Ingestion Split (Documents vs. Records):**
   - Unstructured data (PDFs, raw text): `POST /v1/documents`
   - Structured JSON data: define shape with `POST /v1/schemas`, then store and
     index with `POST /v1/records`

2. **The File Upload Handshake (CRITICAL — never skip this):**
   - Files are NOT sent as multipart/form-data to the API.
   - Step 1: `POST /v1/documents/upload` with filename and indexMode → get `uploadUrl`
   - Step 2: HTTP PUT the raw file bytes directly to `uploadUrl`
   - The system indexes automatically after the PUT completes.

3. **Hybrid Search (BM25 + Vector — RRF fusion):**
   - `POST /v1/search` fuses BM25 text scoring and semantic vector similarity via
     Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). Always default to `mode: HYBRID`.
   - For exact phrase matching, set `textMode: PHRASE`.
   - For broad semantic-only queries, set `mode: SEMANTIC`.

4. **Context Hydration for LLMs (CRITICAL):**
   - Search returns `chunkText` (the exact matched snippet) and `contextText`
     (the broader surrounding passage).
   - Always feed `contextText` to LLM prompts. Using `chunkText` alone causes
     context fragmentation and degrades answer quality.

5. **Identity Idempotency:**
   - `POST /v1/users`, `/v1/orgs`, `/v1/clients` require an `externalId`.
   - POSTing with an existing `externalId` safely returns the existing record —
     no 409 Conflict, safe to retry.

6. **Async Indexing & Webhooks:**
   - Document and record indexing is asynchronous. The resource is created
     immediately but `indexStatus` transitions PENDING_INDEX → INDEXED over seconds.
   - Register webhooks to receive `document.indexed`, `document.failed`,
     `record.indexed`, `record.failed` events.
   - Webhook payloads are signed. Verify using the `X-Vectros-Signature` header
     (Ed25519 or HMAC-SHA256 depending on your webhook configuration).

7. **Pagination:**
   - List, lookup, and version endpoints return a uniform cursor-paginated
     envelope: `{ data, nextCursor }`. Pass `nextCursor` back as the `startFrom`
     query param to fetch the next page; `null` means no more pages.
   - Two endpoints are NOT enveloped: `POST /v1/search` (read `results`) and
     `GET /v1/usage` (returns its payload directly).

## Trust, isolation & compliance

- Every data-plane resource is partitioned by an auth-derived context that fails
  closed — lookups never cross a context boundary.
- Sensitive fields are destroyed at write time and kept out of the search index;
  read-time masking applies unless a caller is explicitly scoped to reveal.
- Read-access logging, GDPR-style erasure and export, and per-customer
  hard-delete are first-class operations.

## Documentation & Full Specification

- **Docs home:** https://docs.vectros.ai/ — concept, how-to, and reference guides,
  plus a docs index for LLMs at https://docs.vectros.ai/llms.txt and the full
  corpus at https://docs.vectros.ai/llms-full.txt
- **Canonical OpenAPI spec:** https://docs.vectros.ai/openapi.yaml — OpenAPI 3.0.
  Never hallucinate endpoints or fields. Always refer to the spec for exact
  payload structures.
- **Rendered API reference:** https://docs.vectros.ai/api-reference/
